executive_summary.first_failure_prediction: Database N+1 queries during daily profile refresh (week 3-4 at 15K creators)

issues.title: N+1 Query Pattern in Daily Profile Sync (sync-orchestrator.ts:74)
issues.description:
syncProfiles() fetches 50 creators, then runs fetchProfiles() which does Apify batch. However, each profile update is a separate .update() call inside loop. At 30K creators with daily refresh:
- Query count: ~30,000 UPDATE calls/day
- Apify API calls: ~600 (batch of 50)
- Supabase cost: ~$0.004/query × 30K = $120/day in excess billing

failure_scenario_6m.week_3_4: First bottleneck detected: syncProfiles reaches 15K creators, 15K sequential UPDATE queries. Supabase CPU spikes to 85%, sync takes 4.5hrs instead of 2hrs. Cron job fails to complete before next run.
failure_scenario_6m.week_5_6: 30K creators now live. syncProfiles now 30K UPDATE queries/day. Supabase storage growing 200GB/month. Query timeout (30s) hits repeatedly. Profile refresh window becomes 8hrs, overlaps with content_crawl.
failure_scenario_6m.week_7_8: Sync cascades fail entirely. Missed profile refreshes for 48hrs cause stale rankings. Creator dashboard reports wrong follower counts. Admin manually reruns sync during peak hours, causing 500ms latencies for user-facing queries.
failure_scenario_6m.month_4_6: Data integrity: 2% of profile updates silently fail due to timeouts, creating orphaned records. Recommend engine tries to compute metrics on stale data. Revenue leak: low engagement creators not flagged for campaigns.

cost_if_breaks.description: Emergency scaling + manual ops + revenue loss

fix_implementation.approach: Implement batch UPDATE via Postgres CASE statement
fix_implementation.validation: Monitor Supabase query count dashboard. Should drop from 30K to 1 query/day for profile batch.
fix_implementation.testing: Load test with 30K creator records. Measure sync duration <2hrs at 100% dataset.

issues.title: Middleware Session Check on Every Request (middleware.ts:33-36)
issues.description:
Middleware calls supabase.auth.getSession() on every protected route request, which makes a DB call to verify session. At 30K active creators each with avg 20 requests/day:
- Daily DB calls for auth: 600K auth checks
- At peak (4PM UTC): 416 RPS on auth queries
- Supabase max sustained: ~300 RPS for free tier

failure_scenario_6m.week_2: First 100 creators sign in. ~2K auth checks/day. No issues yet.
failure_scenario_6m.week_4: 5K creators online. Auth checks: 100K/day. Middleware latency averages 150ms. Some 502 Bad Gateway errors appear.
failure_scenario_6m.week_6: 15K active daily. 300K auth checks/day. Peak hour at 4PM UTC: middleware timeout (30s). Users get 504 Gateway Timeout. Dashboard becomes unusable 4-6PM UTC.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: 30K creators. 600K auth checks/day. Supabase auth table locked 30% of peak hours. Cascading timeout failure: session cache expires, forces fresh DB call, creates queue, everything backs up.
failure_scenario_6m.month_3: Permanent 3-5 second latency on all protected routes during 2-6PM UTC. Creator retention drops 12% due to dashboard slowness.

cost_if_breaks.description: Emergency Supabase upgrade + engineering firefighting + churn

cost_to_fix_now.description: Implement session cache with NextAuth or Supabase SDK caching

fix_implementation.approach: Add HTTP-only cookie-based session caching with 15min refresh window
fix_implementation.validation: Auth DB calls should drop from 600K/day to ~100K/day (only refresh cache 16x/creator)
fix_implementation.testing: Load test 30K concurrent users. Measure middleware latency <50ms at 99th percentile.

issues.title: Sequential Cron Jobs with No Timeout/Isolation (vercel.json + route.ts)
issues.description:
All sync jobs (profile_refresh, content_crawl, shop_orders, competitor_discovery) run sequentially in runFullSync() with no timeout protection. If profile_refresh hangs (Apify API down), the entire sync chain blocks for hours, and subsequent cron windows miss their jobs.

Vercel Functions timeout: 60s for Hobby, 60s for Starter. runFullSync() can take 15-20 min at scale.

failure_scenario_6m.week_3: Apify API experiences brief outage (15 min). syncProfiles() hangs awaiting Apify. Entire sync job fails. No shop orders synced for 6hrs (next cron window). Content refresh missed.
failure_scenario_6m.week_5: During peak Apify usage (other customers), API response time degrades to 45s per batch. runFullSync() takes 35min. Vercel Function timeout hits 60s, execution stops mid-sync. Partial data committed: 200 profiles updated, 0 videos, 0 orders. DB in inconsistent state.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: Competitor discovery adapter hits rate limit (250 req/min). Blocks on HashtagSearch. Entire sync blocked for 2hrs. Content and shop data falls 24hrs behind.
failure_scenario_6m.month_2: Manual daily admin intervention: restart failed cron, check for data gaps. 10 hrs/week engineering ops. Revenue impact: orders synced late, GMV reporting 1-2 days behind, creators see outdated affiliate stats.

cost_if_breaks.description: Manual ops + data inconsistency + lost opportunity data

cost_to_fix_now.description: Implement job isolation, timeouts, and async queuing

fix_implementation.approach: Split runFullSync into independent queued jobs with individual timeouts and error boundaries
fix_implementation.validation: Each job completes independently. If Apify times out, shop_orders still runs. Dashboard shows per-job status.
fix_implementation.testing: Simulate Apify timeout. Verify other jobs complete. Test queue with cascading failures.

issues.title: Missing Database Indexes on Hot Query Paths (schema.sql + RLS policies)
issues.description:
Key queries in sync-orchestrator and middleware lack indexes:
- creators.select() filtering by status + order by last_active_at (syncProfiles:57-62) — no index
- creator_accounts.select() by auth_user_id (middleware:47-50) — has index, OK
- content_tracking.select() by creator_id + posted_at range (computeMetrics:325-330) — no composite index
- referrals.select() by status (implied by bonus calculation logic) — no index

At 300K content_tracking records, range scans become table scans. At 30K creators, full sequential scan on creators table.

failure_scenario_6m.week_2: 300 creators, 3K content_tracking records. Queries still fast (<50ms).
failure_scenario_6m.week_4: 5K creators, 50K content_tracking records. First slowdown observed: computeMetrics queries take 2s. Sync window lengthens.
failure_scenario_6m.week_6: 15K creators, 150K content_tracking records. syncProfiles range scan now 5s. contentTracking queries 8s. Full sync window: 25min.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: 30K creators, 300K records. creators table seq scan: 15s. content_tracking range query: 20s. Sync window: 45min. Timeout risk.
failure_scenario_6m.month_3: Creator dashboard loads slowly (5s) due to creator_recommendations join without index on creator_id. Users report lag.

cost_if_breaks.description: Supabase overages + emergency index rebuild during load + user experience impact

cost_to_fix_now.description: Add indexes to schema before data load

fix_implementation.approach: Add missing indexes to all hot query paths
fix_implementation.validation: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on sync queries. Should show index scans, not seq scans.
fix_implementation.testing: Load test with 300K content_tracking records. Queries should stay <200ms.

issues.title: Apify Adapter Polling Timeout Risk (tiktok-crawler.adapter.ts:76-102)
issues.description:
waitAndGetResults() polls Apify actor status with hardcoded 5s interval, max 5min timeout. If Apify is slow or API has transient issues:
- 5min = 60 polls
- No exponential backoff
- No circuit breaker
- Entire profile batch waits 5min before failing

At 600 batch calls/day, even 1% timeout rate = 6 failed batches/day = 300 creators unsynced.

failure_scenario_6m.week_4: First Apify outage (30min). 3 concurrent syncProfiles() calls pile up. 2 timeout after 5min. Profile data for 300 creators not updated.
failure_scenario_6m.week_6: Apify rate limit kicks in. Some actor runs queue. Polling hits timeout repeatedly. Sync reliability drops to 85%.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: During peak hours (competitors also using Apify heavily), 10-15% of profile fetches timeout. Cascading retry causes 2x API calls.
failure_scenario_6m.month_2: Unpredictable sync success rate. Some creators' data 3-4 days stale. Recommendation engine makes bad suggestions based on old metrics.

cost_if_breaks.description: Extra API calls due to retries + stale data impacting recommendations + engineering debugging

cost_to_fix_now.description: Add exponential backoff, circuit breaker, and adaptive polling

fix_implementation.approach: Add exponential backoff and circuit breaker to Apify polling
fix_implementation.validation: Simulate Apify slowdown. Verify exponential backoff kicks in. Monitor Apify API call count.
fix_implementation.testing: Test with various Apify response times: normal, slow, timeout. Verify circuit breaker behavior.

issues.title: RLS Policy Inefficiency (schema-v2.sql, line 72)
issues.description:
RLS policy for creator_recommendations uses subquery:

WHERE creator_id IN (SELECT creator_id FROM creator_accounts WHERE auth_user_id = auth.uid())

At scale, this subquery is evaluated per row in every query. For a creator viewing their recommendations:
- Query hits creator_recommendations (10-50 rows expected)
- RLS policy runs subquery for EACH row
- Joins creator_accounts for each row evaluation
- Result: 10-50 DB lookups instead of 1 permission check

With 30K creators each viewing dashboard, this cascades to millions of unnecessary subquery executions.

failure_scenario_6m.week_2: 10 creators viewing dashboard. Subquery overhead minimal.
failure_scenario_6m.week_4: 1K creators viewing simultaneously. Each query hits RLS filter 20 times. Query latency: 200ms.
failure_scenario_6m.week_6: 5K concurrent viewers. Subqueries are 60% of query cost. Dashboard takes 800ms.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: 10K concurrent viewers. RLS subquery evaluation becomes bottleneck. Dashboard hangs (3s+). Users report unresponsiveness.
failure_scenario_6m.month_2: Peak hours (4-6PM UTC): 15K+ concurrent creators. RLS CPU spiking. Some dashboard queries timeout.

cost_if_breaks.description: Dashboard performance degradation + potential Supabase overages + churn

cost_to_fix_now.description: Refactor RLS to use direct auth.uid() joins instead of subqueries

fix_implementation.approach: Materialize auth check in policy instead of subquery
fix_implementation.code_change:
Replace RLS policy with materialized join. Create indexed mapping table or use direct auth.uid() check:

-- Better approach: Join directly on auth.uid()
CREATE POLICY 'Creators see own recommendations'
  ON creator_recommendations FOR SELECT TO authenticated
  AS PERMISSIVE
  USING (
    creator_id = (
      SELECT creator_id FROM creator_accounts 
      WHERE auth_user_id = auth.uid() 
      LIMIT 1
    )
  );

-- Even better: Cache mapping in materialized view or denormalize
CREATE INDEX idx_creator_accounts_auth_creator 
  ON creator_accounts(auth_user_id, creator_id);
fix_implementation.validation: Query EXPLAIN ANALYZE on dashboard queries. RLS filter cost should drop from 60% to <5%.
fix_implementation.testing: Load test 10K concurrent dashboard viewers. Latency should stay <200ms.

issues.title: No Caching for Competitor Discovery Results
issues.description:
runCompetitorDiscovery() queries Apify hashtag search every Monday/Thursday, searching 6 brands with 30 hashtags each = 180 API calls. Results are deterministic for 24hrs but not cached.

If discovery runs on schedule, same brands searched repeatedly. No TTL cache on DiscoveredCreator results. Every run rediscovers the same 500 creators.

failure_scenario_6m.week_3: First competitor discovery Monday. Finds 150 new creators. Stored in outreach_pipeline.
failure_scenario_6m.week_5: Discovery runs again. Finds same 150 + 50 new. Old ones already in DB, so 50 net-new. Detected by 'already_in_db' check.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: Over 4 runs, 180 API calls/run = 720 Apify calls for competitor discovery alone. Results 95% redundant.
failure_scenario_6m.month_2: Discovery data becomes stale. No freshness indicator. Suggests contacting creators discovered 6 weeks ago (already converted or churned).

cost_if_breaks.description: Excessive API calls + stale outreach data + engineering investigation

cost_to_fix_now.description: Implement Redis cache or Supabase-backed cache layer

fix_implementation.approach: Add 48hr TTL cache for discovery results, store brand-specific discovery snapshots
fix_implementation.validation: Monitor Apify API call count. Second run of same brands should use cache, 0 API calls.
fix_implementation.testing: Run discovery twice in succession. Verify second run instant (cached). Clear cache, run again, verify API calls.

issues.title: No Rate Limiting or Throttling on Sync API (/api/sync/route.ts)

failure_scenario_6m.week_4: Admin manually triggers 'full' sync twice by mistake. Two runFullSync() instances start. Both spawn Apify jobs. 1200 API calls instead of 600.
failure_scenario_6m.week_6: Monitoring alert misfires, fires 10 consecutive sync requests. CPU/API quotas exhausted. Legitimate scheduled cron jobs fail.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: Accidental script loops and fires 100 sync requests. Apify rate-limited. Supabase CPU maxed. All creators data stale for 6hrs.

cost_if_breaks.description: Apify rate limiting + Supabase compute exhaustion + manual ops + data staleness

cost_to_fix_now.description: Add rate limiting and concurrency control

fix_implementation.approach: Add request-level rate limiting and global sync concurrency lock
fix_implementation.validation: Try to spam requests. Verify 429 response after 5 requests/hour.
fix_implementation.testing: Load test with rapid successive sync requests. Verify only one executes at a time.

issues.title: Potential Data Loss in Upsert Without Conflict Handling (sync-orchestrator.ts:146)
issues.description:
content_tracking.upsert() uses onConflict: 'video_id', but if a video_id already exists with different creator_id (data corruption scenario), the upsert silently overwrites. No INSERT OR IGNORE fallback, no duplicate key detection.

Also, batch size for content crawl is 30 creators. If Apify rate-limits mid-batch, only N creators' videos are synced, rest silently skipped due to error handling.

failure_scenario_6m.week_5: Data corruption: same video_id assigned to 2 creators (crawler bug). First upsert creates record. Second upsert overwrites creator_id. Content attributed to wrong creator.
failure_scenario_6m.week_6: batch loop continues on crawler errors (line 189: catch { failed++ }), so if videos fail to fetch for creator #20, #21-30 still try to fetch but earlier data was partially committed.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: Multiple content_tracking records in DB with mismatched creator_id from earlier corruption. Metrics compute reports wrong GMV aggregations.

cost_if_breaks.description: Data reconciliation + metric recomputation + potential fraud detection false positives

cost_to_fix_now.description: Add validation and error handling to upsert operations

fix_implementation.approach: Add constraint validation and atomic batch operations
fix_implementation.validation: Verify no cross-creator video assignments in DB. Add integrity check query.
fix_implementation.testing: Simulate duplicate video_id scenarios. Verify error handling and logging.

issues.title: No Metrics Staleness Tracking or Warning System
issues.description: RecommendationEngine queries creator_recommendations (line 56-60) which were last computed via computeMetrics(). No timestamp on when metrics were computed. If sync fails for 2 days, recommendations are based on 2-day-old data, but dashboard doesn't warn the creator.

failure_scenario_6m.week_5: Sync fails for 24hrs (Apify outage). Creator sees recommendation 'improve hook' based on yesterday's avg_views. Actual views are 50% lower now.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: Sync fails for 48hrs. Recommendations stale. Creator acts on false signal. Posts suboptimal content based on stale data.
failure_scenario_6m.month_2: Periodic Apify slowdowns cause data to be 1-3 days old. Creator trust in recommendations drops.

cost_if_breaks.description: Erosion of recommendation credibility + lost content quality opportunity

fix_implementation.approach: Add computed_at timestamp to recommendations; warn if >24hrs stale
fix_implementation.validation: Dashboard shows 'Last computed X hours ago'
fix_implementation.testing: Mock stale timestamp. Verify warning appears.

issues.title: Shipping Tracker Integration Lacks Error Recovery (shipping-tracker.adapter.ts incomplete)
issues.description: AfterShipTrackingAdapter.trackBatch() not shown in provided code, but implies batch API call to track multiple shipments. If 1 tracking number fails, entire batch might fail. No graceful degradation.

failure_scenario_6m.week_6: Sample shipment batch includes invalid tracking number. AfterShip API returns 400 for one record. Entire batch operation fails. No shipments update their status.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: Multiple invalid tracking numbers accumulate. Sample reminders don't trigger because delivery status not updated.

cost_if_breaks.description: Missed reminder sends + sample tracking failure

cost_to_fix_now.description: Not urgent. Implement error handling per item, not per batch.

fix_implementation.approach: Implement per-item error handling in trackBatch()
fix_implementation.validation: Include invalid tracking in batch. Verify valid ones succeed, invalid logged but don't fail batch.
fix_implementation.testing: Mock AfterShip API with mixed success/failure responses.

issues.title: Hardcoded Credentials in ENV Variables Without Rotation Strategy
issues.description: TikTok Shop API credentials (appKey, appSecret, accessToken) stored in env. No key rotation schedule. No expiry tracking for accessToken.

failure_scenario_6m.month_4: TikTok rotates API key. Application fails to sync orders for 24hrs until deployment with new key.

cost_to_fix_now.description: Not urgent. Plan key rotation workflow.

fix_implementation.approach: Implement key rotation warning system post-launch
fix_implementation.code_change: Document: Set quarterly reminder to check TikTok Shop API key expiry.

issues.title: No Observability for Sync Job Health Metrics
issues.description:
Sync jobs run but no monitoring dashboard tracking:
- Success/failure rates per job type
- Job duration trends
- API quota consumption (Apify, Supabase)
- Data freshness (last sync timestamp per creator)

No way to proactively detect degradation until users report issues.

failure_scenario_6m.week_6: Apify API quota silently nearing limit. No alerting. Admin notices by accident when sync slows down.
failure_scenario_6m.week_8: Shop order sync success rate drops from 100% to 92%. No dashboard metric. Orders silently missing.

cost_to_fix_now.description: Not critical for MVP. Implement post-launch.

fix_implementation.approach: Add sync metrics table and dashboard post-launch

critical_path_fixes.timeline: Deploy all before week 2 (start of data ramp)
critical_path_fixes.impact: Prevents cascading failures in profile sync, auth, and job orchestration

high_priority_follow_up.impact: Ensures API resilience, RLS performance, and sync stability

deferred_improvements.impact: Data integrity and error recovery enhancements

monitoring_tasks.timeline: Set up dashboards during launch week

most_likely_failure.mitigation: Session caching reduces DB load by 80%

cascading_failure_risk.cascade: If profile sync blocks, content_crawl + shop_orders miss windows → data staleness → revenue impact

week_0_preflight: ARCH-008: Add rate limiting to /api/sync (1 day)
post_launch: ARCH-010: Set up staleness tracking (1 day, low priority)
ARCH-013: Build sync observability dashboard (3 days, medium priority)

success_metrics: Sync job duration <5 min for 5K creators
Zero cascading failures in cron jobs
Sync job duration <15 min for 15K creators
RLS policy cost <5% of dashboard query
